edit.pinHelp: Show this saved item on the Home dashboard.
edit.calculationLockedBody: Loan type and amount stay fixed on saved items. Create a new calculation if you want to model a different amount or switch between loan and mortgage tracking.
edit.loanInputsLockedBody: Saved loan inputs are kept as the original calculation snapshot. Create a new calculation if you want to model a different scenario.

about.title: About
about.formulaDesc: LoanBee uses the standard annuity formula to calculate monthly payments:
about.varP: P = Principal (loan amount after down payment)
about.varN: n = Total number of payments (term in months)
about.disclaimer: This calculator is for illustrative purposes only. Always consult a qualified financial advisor before making borrowing decisions.
about.footer: LoanBee - Made with the calm precision of a finance workspace.

faqItems.a: Loan amortisation is the systematic repayment of a loan over time through regular instalments. Each payment is usually split between principal and interest. Early payments often contain more interest because the balance is high, then more of each payment goes toward principal as the balance falls.

An amortisation schedule shows how each payment may be split between principal and interest, and how the balance may reduce over time. It is useful for planning, but actual lender schedules can differ because of fees, variable rates, rounding, or lender-specific rules.

faqItems.q: Should I make extra payments to pay off a loan quicker?
faqItems.a: Extra payments can reduce the principal balance faster, which may lower the total interest paid and shorten the repayment time. Before overpaying, consider emergency savings, other high-interest debt, retirement saving, and whether your lender charges early repayment fees.

faqItems.q: Things to consider when calculating extra loan payments
faqItems.a: Consider your budget, high-interest debt, emergency fund, retirement goals, and whether professional advice would help. Extra payments can be useful, but they should fit your wider financial plan.

Principal is the amount you borrow. Interest is the extra cost charged by the lender. Term is how long you take to repay the loan. Monthly payment is what you pay each month. Balance is what you still owe.

faqItems.q: What are the two calculator modes?
faqItems.a: By Term means you enter the loan length and the calculator works out the monthly payment. By Payment means you enter the monthly payment you want and the calculator works out how long the loan could take to repay.

guide.title: Start with your loan, then explore what could change.
guide.body: LoanBee keeps the maths out of the way so you can try numbers, compare options and save what matters.

details.title: Add your loan details
details.body: Enter the amount, rate and term.

compare.title: Compare your options
compare.body: See the monthly payment and how overpayments could help.

save.title: Save and revisit
save.body: Keep a loan, update it later and track progress.

guide.inviteBody: Start with what you know. You can adjust the numbers whenever you like.

disclaimer.label: Disclaimer:
disclaimer.shortText: Results are estimates and do not constitute financial advice.
disclaimer.fullText: This app provides general information and calculation tools for educational purposes only. Nothing in this app constitutes financial, legal, tax, or investment advice. All calculations and examples are illustrative and may not reflect your personal circumstances. Always consult a qualified financial adviser before making financial decisions. The Tech Narrative Ltd. accepts no liability for decisions made based on information or tools provided in this app.
disclaimer.text: For illustrative purposes only. Not financial advice.

errors.loanAmount: Loan amount must be greater than 0
errors.interest: Interest rate must be greater than 0
errors.interestMax: Interest rate must be less than or equal to 100
errors.termYearInvalid: Term in years must be a non-negative whole number
errors.termTooLong: Term cannot be longer than 100 years
errors.termMonthRange: Term in months must be between 0 and 11
errors.termRequired: Term in years and months cannot both be 0
errors.downPaymentNonNegative: Down payment must be greater than or equal to 0
errors.downPaymentCash: Down payment cannot exceed the loan amount
errors.desiredPaymentNonNegative: Desired monthly payment must be greater than or equal to 0
errors.desiredPaymentRequired: Desired monthly payment must be greater than 0
errors.desiredPaymentExceeds: Desired monthly payment cannot exceed the loan balance
errors.desiredPaymentMinimum: Desired monthly payment must be at least {{amount}} to reduce the balance
errors.additionalPaymentNonNegative: Additional monthly payment must be greater than or equal to 0
errors.additionalPaymentExceeds: Additional monthly payment cannot exceed the loan balance

errorBoundary.title: Something went wrong
errorBoundary.message: The app ran into an unexpected error. Tap below to try again — your saved loans are safe.

forms.requiredPositive: Enter a value greater than zero
forms.requiredNonNegative: Enter a value of zero or more
forms.invalidInteger: Enter a whole number of zero or more
forms.durationTooLong: Enter a duration no longer than the total mortgage term
forms.interestMax: Enter a rate of 100% or less

loan.renameLoanHelp: This only changes the name shown in LoanBee. Calculation figures and currency stay unchanged.

mortgage.renameMortgageHelp: This only changes the name shown in LoanBee. Deal figures and currency stay unchanged.
mortgage.dashboardHint: Save a calculation, then pin it to your dashboard to track progress.
mortgage.dashboardSwipeHint: Swipe sideways to view pinned items
mortgage.sourceProjectedFromOlderBankCheckpoint: Projected from older bank check-in on {{date}}
mortgage.sourceProjectedFromStaleBankCheckpoint: Projected from stale bank check-in on {{date}}; re-check recommended
mortgage.currentStateProjectionBody: This estimate uses the balance, rate, term, and payment assumptions you saved. Past deals are not needed.
mortgage.savedMortgageEstimateBody: Based on the figures you saved. Add deal changes only when you want to model a planned remortgage, rate switch, or payment change.
mortgage.noDealChangesBody: This mortgage is saved from the figures you entered. You do not need to recreate past deals; add deal changes later only if you want to plan a future rate, term, or payment change.
mortgage.draftExcludedFromEstimateBody: {{name}} is a draft. It is not included in balances, charts, totals, or payoff dates until it becomes active.
mortgage.draftPreviewBody: Drafts are not included in balances, charts, totals, or payoff dates until they become active.
mortgage.unpinHint: Visible on your Home dashboard. Tap to unpin.
mortgage.futureStartHelp: Progress and activity appear once this mortgage has started.
mortgage.openingBalanceHintBody: If you've already been paying this mortgage, tap your deal to set the bank-confirmed opening balance.
mortgage.newCalculationHelp: Start a fresh calculation on the home screen.
mortgage.recordBalanceHelp: Use the latest bank-confirmed mortgage balance.
mortgage.addOverpaymentHelp: Log a one-off extra payment against this deal.
mortgage.addNextDealHelp: Draft or publish the next lender deal.
mortgage.completeCurrentDealHelp: Close this deal with lender-confirmed figures.
mortgage.editCurrentDealHelp: Update rate, payment, dates, or balance details.
mortgage.editDraftDealHelp: Review the draft or delete it before publishing.
mortgage.bankBalanceTruth: Use the amount your lender says you owed. Bank-confirmed balances are the source of truth for mortgage tracking.
mortgage.monthlyPaymentAutoHelp: Calculated automatically from the opening balance, rate, repayment type, and fixed overall mortgage term.
mortgage.dealStartLockedHint: Start date is set automatically from the previous deal.
mortgage.dealDurationHint: How long this deal runs before remortgaging or moving to a new product.
mortgage.deleteDraftMessage: This removes the future deal draft and any events attached to it. This cannot be undone.
mortgage.deleteDealMessage: This removes {{name}} and any activity logged against it. This cannot be undone.
mortgage.recalculateLaterDealsMessage: Saving this deal will update opening balances for {{count}} later deal(s) using the recalculated closing balance.
mortgage.recalculateLaterDealsMessage_one: Saving this deal will update the opening balance for {{count}} later deal using the recalculated closing balance.
mortgage.recalculateLaterDealsMessage_few: Saving this deal will update opening balances for {{count}} later deals using the recalculated closing balance.
mortgage.recalculateLaterDealsMessage_many: Saving this deal will update opening balances for {{count}} later deals using the recalculated closing balance.
mortgage.recalculateLaterDealsMessage_other: Saving this deal will update opening balances for {{count}} later deals using the recalculated closing balance.
mortgage.correctCompletedMessage: This will remove {{deals}} later deal(s) and {{events}} event(s) because their balances depend on this completed deal.
mortgage.invalidDealDates: Enter a start date and an end date after the start date.
mortgage.invalidDealAmounts: Enter a bank balance, interest rate, and monthly payment greater than zero.
mortgage.invalidCompletionDetails: Enter a completion date and a closing balance of zero or more.
mortgage.cannotPublishMessage: Complete the existing deal with bank-confirmed closing details before starting this one.
mortgage.completeBeforeNewDealMessage: You can draft the next deal now, but it cannot be published or become active until the current deal is completed with your lender's closing balance.
mortgage.draftOnlyUntilCompleted: This deal can only be saved as a draft until the current deal is completed.
mortgage.editCompletedMessage: Changing a completed deal will recalculate later mortgage estimates while preserving bank-confirmed balances.
mortgage.completeDealHelp: Enter the final figures from your lender. This reconciles the deal and allows the next deal to start.
mortgage.closingBankBalanceHint: Enter the balance your lender shows — including any fees they've rolled in. We'll work the fees out for you.
mortgage.predictedBalanceHint: What LoanBee expects on this date with no fees. The gap to your bank's balance is treated as fees added.
mortgage.feesAddedHint: Arrangement or product fees added to the closing balance — separate from any new borrowing on the next deal.
mortgage.feesAutoHint: Auto-filled from the gap between your bank's balance and our prediction. Edit if your lender itemises fees differently.
mortgage.additionalBorrowingHint: Add only if you're borrowing more than your current balance — e.g. a remortgage that releases equity.
mortgage.totalMortgageTermHint: Adjust if you've extended or shortened your overall mortgage (e.g. 35y → 40y).
mortgage.initialDealLockedBody: Once a second deal exists, the initial deal is locked. Delete later deals if you need to edit it again.
mortgage.dealLockedByLaterBody: Only the latest deal can be edited. Delete later deals until this one is latest if its figures need changing.
mortgage.completedEventReadOnly: This activity belongs to a completed deal. Correct the completed deal if the historic figures need changing.
mortgage.eventHelp: Log notable events during the active deal. You do not need to enter every monthly payment.
mortgage.varianceReasonHelp: This helps explain the difference, but the bank balance is still saved either way.
mortgage.reconciliationBankHigher: Bank balance was {{amount}} higher than our estimate.
mortgage.reconciliationBankLower: Bank balance was {{amount}} lower than our estimate.
mortgage.reconciliationSavingsCaution: Future estimates use your bank balance. Savings remain estimates based on the overpayments you entered.
mortgage.addNoteHelp: Save a dated note against the active deal.
mortgage.missedPaymentHelp: Mark a month where the scheduled payment was missed.
mortgage.deleteEventMessage: This removes the event from your mortgage activity and recalculates projections. This cannot be undone.
mortgage.eventEditHelp: Changes update the mortgage projection immediately.
mortgage.eventRequiresActiveDeal: Activities can only be saved against the current active deal.
mortgage.eventOutsideDealDates: Choose a date within the current deal dates.
mortgage.invalidEventAmount: Enter an overpayment amount greater than zero.
mortgage.invalidEventBalance: Enter a bank-confirmed balance greater than zero.
mortgage.overpaymentTooLarge: The overpayment cannot exceed the current projected balance.
mortgage.couldPaySoonerTitle: Could you pay it off sooner?
mortgage.couldPaySoonerBody: Add a regular monthly overpayment to this deal to reduce your balance faster and save on interest.
mortgage.dealOverpaymentDateNote: Overpayments must fall within this deal's dates. Any lump sum after this deal ends can be added as a deposit on your next deal to reduce its opening balance.
mortgage.warningGapMessage: There is a gap between one deal ending and the next deal starting.
mortgage.warningOverlapMessage: Two mortgage deals overlap. Adjust the start or end dates before publishing.
mortgage.warningIncompleteActiveDealMessage: Enter your bank-confirmed closing balance before starting the next deal.
mortgage.warningDraftBlockedMessage: Complete the current deal with lender figures before this draft can become active.
